chapters.title: What is Software Engineering?
chapters.content: Software engineering is the discipline of designing, building, testing, and maintaining software systems in a reliable and scalable way. It combines coding with planning, teamwork, testing, version control, and continuous improvement.

chapters.title: Version Control with Git
chapters.content: Version control helps teams track code changes over time. Git allows you to create commits, branches, and merges. This makes collaboration safer and easier because everyone can work in parallel while preserving project history.

chapters.title: Client-Server Architecture
chapters.content: Client-server architecture splits responsibilities between clients (frontend apps) and servers (backend services). The client handles user interface and interactions, while the server manages business logic, data, and security.

chapters.title: Introduction to AI
chapters.content: Artificial Intelligence is the field of building systems that can perform tasks requiring human-like intelligence, such as understanding language, recognizing patterns, and making predictions.

chapters.title: What is Machine Learning?
chapters.content: Machine learning is a subset of AI where models learn patterns from data instead of being programmed with fixed rules. With enough data and training, models can predict or classify new inputs.

chapters.title: What is DevOps?
chapters.content: DevOps is a culture and practice that brings development and operations together to deliver software faster and more reliably using automation, collaboration, and continuous feedback.

chapters.title: CI/CD Basics
chapters.content: CI/CD automates integration, testing, and delivery. Continuous Integration catches issues early, while Continuous Delivery helps teams release updates quickly and safely.

chapters.title: Building with LLMs
chapters.content: AI engineering focuses on integrating language models into practical products through prompt design, retrieval, evaluation, and system orchestration.

chapters.title: Evaluation and Safety
chapters.content: Reliable AI products require quality checks for accuracy, consistency, and safety using test sets, guardrails, and continuous monitoring.

chapters.title: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
chapters.content: Cloud service models define responsibility boundaries. IaaS provides infrastructure, PaaS provides managed runtime platforms, and SaaS delivers complete applications.

chapters.title: Scalability and Reliability
chapters.content: Cloud systems scale with demand using load balancing and autoscaling, while reliability is improved through redundancy, backups, and fault tolerance.

chapters.title: End-to-End Web Architecture
chapters.content: Full stack development connects user interfaces, backend APIs, databases, and deployment workflows to deliver complete web applications.

chapters.title: State, APIs, and Data Flow
chapters.content: Modern full stack apps use predictable state management and clear API boundaries so data flows reliably between frontend and backend.

chapters.title: Descriptive and Diagnostic Analytics
chapters.content: Descriptive analytics explains what happened, and diagnostic analytics explores why it happened by drilling into trends and relationships in data.

chapters.title: KPIs and Dashboards
chapters.content: Strong dashboards track actionable KPIs and present visual summaries that support faster and better decision-making.

chapters.title: Prompt Structure
chapters.content: Good prompts include role, context, constraints, and output format to reduce ambiguity and improve model response quality.

chapters.title: Few-Shot and Iterative Prompting
chapters.content: Few-shot prompting uses examples to shape output behavior, and iterative refinement helps improve clarity, tone, and correctness.

chapters.title: From Requirements to Architecture
chapters.content: Solution design translates business needs into technical architecture, balancing scalability, cost, maintainability, and delivery speed.

chapters.title: Trade-Off Analysis
chapters.content: Architects evaluate trade-offs like performance versus cost or speed versus reliability to choose practical implementation approaches.

chapters.title: Planning and Scope
chapters.content: Project planning defines goals, scope, timelines, and resources so teams can execute with clear expectations and reduced risk.

chapters.title: Agile Delivery
chapters.content: Agile methods deliver value incrementally through short iterations, team collaboration, and regular feedback from stakeholders.

chapters.title: Test Pyramid
chapters.content: The test pyramid recommends more unit tests, fewer integration tests, and selective end-to-end tests for fast and reliable quality feedback.

chapters.title: UI and API Automation
chapters.content: Automation frameworks validate application behavior by testing API contracts and critical user flows, improving release confidence.
